steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says Moss 'had never tired of this specific view.' The blank describes the Port of Oakland view he is singling out. The word must mean something like 'distinct' or 'specific' — identifying one view among many rather than views in general.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Imaginary: means existing only in the mind. The view is a real place he has looked at repeatedly. ✗
  • Choice B — Correct: means accurate or right. There is no sense of correctness involved in singling out a view. ✗
  • Choice C — Energetic: means full of energy. This describes a feeling, not the act of identifying a specific instance. ✗
  • Choice D — Particular: means a distinct, definite instance being singled out. 'This particular view' captures exactly the idea that Moss has chosen this one view over all others. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
